context: =  acute / chronic inflammation of the myocardium Cause: Sequela:   dilated cardiomyopathy (in up to 10%) Echocardiography (low sensitivity): √  normal heart function or global / regional LV hypokinesis MR: √  patchy diffuse / nodular hyperenhancement in nonvascular distribution up to 4 weeks after infection ← inflammation and cell necrosis Location:   inferior and inferolateral myocardial segments Site:  subepicardial / midmyocardial √  increased T2 signal √  wall motion abnormalities in affected regions Benefit:   biopsy guidance to areas of higher pathologic yield PET/CT: √  increased metab
